key_insight: A D-gun applied Al2O3-TiO2 coating significantly improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest best predicts wear from test conditions.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical surface-engineering route for extending the service life of titanium components in aerospace, biomedical, and marine applications.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 coating on Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spraying.
Evaluated tribological performance via ASTM G99 pin-on-disc tests at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Trained ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from sliding parameters.
evidence_signal: SEM and wear testing showed lower wear and formation of a protective tribo-layer on coated samples, while random forest had the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error.
watch_item: Performance depends on tightly controlled D-gun parameters and the trained model is only validated over the reported load/speed/time range.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
